title: silene vulgaris subsp. suffrutescens
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. suffrutescens, also known as a sub-species of the Bladder Campion, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Silene vulgaris subsp. suffrutescens include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to ease respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
